Support for Unicode in DB2 Connector

Support for Unicode in DB2 Connector

The Uniface DB2 connector enables Unicode support (with some limitations). By default, Unicode support is enabled.

The full range of Unicode characters supported by DB2 can be stored in fields that use W and C packing codes, regardless of whether the database was created to use Unicode (UTF-8 encoding) or another code page.

With Unicode enabled, the connector works in Unicode mode, so Unicode characters can be used in:

  • Fields with wide packing codes (such as W40, W*, SW100)
  • Fields with character packing codes (such as C40, C*, SC100)
  • Table names and column names (except in non-Unicode databases)
  • SQL Workbench and the sql ProcScript statement
  • Parameters of stored procedures

Unicode support is limited in a non-Unicode DB2 database.

Note: DB2 on the iSeries does not use the concept of a Unicode database, so databases on iSeries are considered to be non-Unicode databases, with added UTF16 support.

Unicode in Unicode Databases

For a Unicode database, the $DEF_CHARSET assignment setting must be set to UTF8. This ensures that:

  • Both W and C packing code are stored as Unicode in the database, and the full range of Unicode characters supported by DB2 can be stored in both types of fields, including SW and SC fields.
  • Entity and field names can contain any Unicode character. Tables created on- the-fly will have the correct Unicode name in the database.

Unicode in Non-Unicode Databases on Windows and Unix

To enable Unicode in a non-Unicode DB2 database on Windows and Unix, you need to set the alt_collate configuration parameter. Otherwise, the CCSID UNICODE clause cannot be added to table definitions.

If $DEF_CHARSET is UTF8

  • Both W and C packing code are stored as Unicode in the database, and the full range of Unicode characters supported by DB2 can be stored in both types of fields. On DB2 9.7 and higher, this also applies to SW and SC fields. In lower versions, SW and SC fields can only contain characters from the database code page.
  • Entity and field names can only contain characters from the database code page. Otherwise, runtime errors will be the result, and, if a table is created, the name will contain substitution characters.
  • CCSID UNICODE is added to all table definitions that contain a field with a C or W packing code, and the C and W packing codes are treated identically on Windows and Unix: the SQL data type will be CHAR, VARCHAR or CLOB with CCSID Unicode, which is UTF8.

If $DEF_CHARSET is not set to UTF8, when an entity contains a field with a W packing code, CCSID UNICODE is added to the table definition. This turns the whole table into Unicode, including fields that use a C packing code. In this case, the C fields are still Unicode but only characters that occur in the code page defined by the $DEF_CHARSET assignment setting can be used.

Unicode in Non-Unicode Databases on iSeries

If $DEF_CHARSET is UTF8:

  • The W packing code is stored as UTF16, with a CCSID of 1200.
  • The C packing code is stored as specified in $DEF_CHARSET. If it is UTF8, the C packing code is stored as UTF8 with a CCSID of 1208
  • Entity and field names can only contain characters from the database code page. Otherwise, runtime errors will occur, and if a table is created, the name will contain substitution characters.
  • The C packing code is stored in a CHAR, VARCHAR or CLOB field with CCSID 1208, which is UTF8. The W packing code is stored in a GRAPHIC, VARGRAPHIC or DBCLOB field with CCSID 1200, which is UTF16.

If $DEF_CHARSET is not set to UTF8, it is possible to specify the CCSID per column, so if an entity contains a field with a W packing code, this does not affect fields with the C packing code; these fields still conform to $DEF_CHARSET.

Limitations

  • On Windows and Unix, if a Uniface application uses paths to more than one DB2 databases, all DB2 databases must be either Unicode or non-Unicode databases, except in the following circumstances:

    • Unicode support is switched off (using the unicode connector option)
    • The W packing code is not used at all
    • Tables are not created on-the-fly by the connector.

    Otherwise, tables may be created on-the-fly with the wrong data type for the W fields (GRAPHIC in a non-Unicode database, or CHAR in a Unicode database).

  • In DB2 9.6 and lower, the contents of SW fields (that is, CLOB fields in the database) are limited to characters that exist in the database code page, even when the table was created with CCSID UNICODE. This is a DB2 limitation.

  • On iSeries, segmented fields with the SW packing code are stored in a field with data type DBCLOB, CCSID 1200. Unfortunately, there are some problems in i5/OS and IBM i6 that require you to install a PTF in order to make them work:

    • For i5/OS V5R4 PTF SI30087 or a superseding one must be installed, otherwise DBCLOB fields with CCSID 1200 cannot be properly retrieved.
    • For IBM I 6, PTF SI43229 or a superseding one must be installed, otherwise DBCLOB fields with CCSID 1200 cannot be properly retrieved.
Related Links
  • Uniface Support for Unicode

    Uniface supports Unicode in the user interface, database storage and retrieval, sorting, case conversion, and data exchange. Uniface uses Unicode UTF-8 encoding for as its internal character set.

  • Configuring DB2 for Unicode

    Edit the application assignment file and database configuration files to configure the DB2 connector for Unicode.

  • Unicode Packing Codes

    When Unicode is enabled, Uniface W and C packing codes can contain Unicode characters. The precise mapping and the number of Unicode characters that a field can contain depend on whether the DB2 database was created to use Unicode (UTF-8 encoding) or not (a code page).

  • $DEF_CHARSET

    Specify the default character set used for storing data in String fields with C packing code.

  • $SYS_CHARSET

    Specify the character set used for communication with the operating system and for communication between Uniface and non-Uniface components, if they are not Unicode-based.

  • unicode

    Enable or disable Unicode support.
